name: Lamb with Artichokes and Preserved Lemons
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the fat off the lamb.
2. Cut the meat into 1 1/2-inch chunks.
3. In a 5-6 quart pan, combine the lamb, onion, garlic, ginger, turmeric, and saffron.
4. Cook, tightly covered, over medium heat for 30 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, in a bowl, combine vinegar and 1 quart water. Trim off stems, tough outer leaves, and sharp tips of artichokes, leaving the pale, tender inner leaves. Cut each artichoke in half lengthwise and scoop out the hairy choke; discard it.
6. Immerse the trimmed artichokes in the vinegar-water mixture.
7. After 30 minutes, turn off the heat and uncover the pan. Stir often until juices evaporate and meat browns, about 15-20 minutes.
8. Stir in 2 cups water and simmer, covered, for 1 hour.
9. Drain the artichokes and add them to the lamb. Simmer, covered, for an additional 20 minutes.
10. Add 8 preserved lemon quarters and olives; continue simmering, covered, until artichokes are tender when pierced, about 10 more minutes.
11. Stir in 2 tablespoons of liquid from the preserved lemons or lemon juice.
12. Skim off any fat that rises to the surface and discard it.
13. Pour the stew into a bowl and garnish with the remaining lemon quarters.
